title: "Usb disk, fstab and udev."
date: 2009-11-08
forum: Hardware

### Post by jmlaguiness on 2009-11-08
Hello all :D

I'd like my usb disk to mount at starting ubuntu and when i plug it.

(It's /dev/sdb1)

So, i edited these files :

/etc/fstab :
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=899ff4d7-6f14-4d55-9a9b-69ba2139b107 / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=0ECEDAAF12E682FB /media/350Go ntfs-3g defaults,locale=fr_FR.UTF-8 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=84566FC1566FB31A /media/win ntfs-3g defaults,locale=fr_FR.UTF-8 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sdb1 :
UUID=52A076C8A076B259      /media/1To     ntfs-3g     defaults,noauto,rw    0    1

```The udev rule file : /lib/udev/rules.d/50-udev-default.rules

```

ACTION=="add", KERNEL=="sd[b-e]*", ATTR{model}=="HD103UJ", RUN+="/bin/mount /dev/%k"
ACTION=="remove", KERNEL=="sd[b-e]*", ATTR{model}=="HD103UJ", RUN+="/bin/umount -l /dev/%k"

```

But it doesn't work at all : the disk isn't when i boot, and if i try to mount it "manually" clicking on Media* in the shorcut menu, it say me that only root can mount the disk...

I've to mount it as root in terminal :'(...

Any idea ?

Thank you :D
